title: Oprah's Baby Daddy

jokes.text: Like I'll be with my kids and people be like, 'I'm Rick James, bitch.'
jokes.setup: People yelling references to his show when he's with his children
jokes.punchline: The absurd inappropriateness of strangers yelling 'Rick James, bitch' at a family man
jokes.explanation: Solid observational comedy about the weird disconnect between Dave's comedy persona and real life as a father

jokes.text: Bicycle. Monkey. Ashy Larry.
jokes.setup: Frank pitching an elaborate movie idea while Dave zones out
jokes.punchline: Dave's mind wandering to completely random thoughts
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly economical representation of a wandering mind - three random words that perfectly capture not paying attention

jokes.text: I wonder what Arsenio's doing right now. He probably at a wine and cheese party.
jokes.setup: Continuing the internal monologue bit
jokes.punchline: Specific, random speculation about Arsenio Hall's current activities
jokes.explanation: Absurd but specific image that plays on stereotypes about Hollywood social events

jokes.text: How come you didn't tell me how good the cheese was? Motherfucker. Some good-ass cheese.
jokes.setup: Dave's mind wandering to imaginary scenarios
jokes.punchline: The absurd aggression over not being told about cheese quality
jokes.explanation: The passionate anger about cheese creates an absurd and memorable moment

jokes.text: Bob Eubanks-lookin' motherfucker, you should've told me how good the cheese was!
jokes.setup: Continuing the cheese rage fantasy
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific Bob Eubanks reference as an insult
jokes.explanation: Bob Eubanks is such a random reference that it becomes funny through sheer specificity

jokes.text: How you gonna rip it like this, son, how you gonna rip it like that, son
jokes.setup: Dave fantasizing about being a rapper while Frank keeps talking
jokes.punchline: The terrible, generic rap lyrics Dave imagines
jokes.explanation: Mildly funny parody of generic rap lyrics, but not particularly clever

jokes.text: $12,000,000! (dinging register)
jokes.setup: Frank offering $12 million for the movie
jokes.punchline: Dave's mind suddenly snaps to attention with a cash register sound effect
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - the cash register sound represents the exact moment greed overrides artistic integrity

jokes.text: Waitress, get this nigga something to drink.
jokes.setup: Dave suddenly being interested after hearing the money
jokes.punchline: The instant personality shift to accommodating host
jokes.explanation: Shows Dave's character shift from dismissive to welcoming once money is involved

jokes.text: (thinking) Nascar race. Unicorns. Dirty diapers.
jokes.setup: Fan continues pitching while Dave pretends to listen
jokes.punchline: More random thoughts showing he's still not paying attention
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the wandering mind bit, maintains the joke structure

jokes.text: Dave, it's Oprah. Look Dave, I'm pregnant. Are you sure it's mine? No, Dave, listen, it's yours. Got ya bitch!
jokes.setup: Serious pregnancy conversation setup
jokes.punchline: Revealing it's a prank call with 'Got ya bitch!'
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - sets up like a serious paternity issue then reveals it's just Dave prank calling

jokes.text: can you have a limousine pick me up at the baggage claim, because I don't have no cab money
jokes.setup: Dave supposedly being Oprah's baby daddy
jokes.punchline: Still being broke enough to need cab fare
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - even in this fantasy, Dave is still broke

jokes.text: Kiss my ass, everybody! Excuse me, can I have a quick word with you? Ah, kiss my ass.
jokes.setup: Dave quitting his job dramatically
jokes.punchline: Telling everyone to kiss his ass, including when his boss tries to talk
jokes.explanation: Standard 'telling the boss off' fantasy but executed with good energy

jokes.text: Hey, everybody, I've got an announcement to make! Ding, ding, ding ding, ding! I quit!
jokes.setup: Dave arriving at work after the phone call
jokes.punchline: Dramatic announcement with bell sounds before quitting
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with the bell sounds, shows character's immediate life change

jokes.text: it's not what's gotten into me, it's what's gotten into Oprah: My seed, son!
jokes.setup: Colleague asking what's gotten into Dave
jokes.punchline: The crude double entendre about impregnating Oprah
jokes.explanation: Effective crude wordplay that's appropriately cringey for the character

jokes.text: I'm rich, bitch!
jokes.setup: Dave celebrating his supposed fortune
jokes.punchline: Using his own Rick James catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective callback that became a cultural phenomenon

jokes.text: remember your spirit. (farts) Oh, it sprayed.
jokes.setup: Dave trying to be philosophical and spiritual
jokes.punchline: Immediately farting and commenting on the spray
jokes.explanation: Basic toilet humor that undercuts the pretentious moment but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: Baby, hey, baby, you mind if I get a happy ending? She didn't say no.
jokes.setup: Dave getting a massage while pregnant Oprah is there
jokes.punchline: Asking for sexual services and justifying Oprah's silence as consent
jokes.explanation: Appropriately cringey behavior showing Dave taking advantage of the situation

jokes.text: $7,000 an hour. Sounds expensive, but I think the safety of you and our baby is priceless
jokes.setup: Dave hiring expensive security
jokes.punchline: Justifying the ridiculous expense with fake concern
jokes.explanation: Shows Dave's character spending Oprah's money frivolously while pretending it's for her benefit

jokes.text: Give it up for my baby's momma, Oprah motherfucking Winfrey
jokes.setup: Dave hosting a party at Oprah's house
jokes.punchline: The crude introduction of Oprah as his 'baby's momma'
jokes.explanation: Effective cringe comedy showing Dave's complete lack of class in the situation

jokes.text: Look, Stedman, you gonna have to move on, alright? No, fuck that, I don't care what y'all had. She's having my baby, okay?
jokes.setup: Stedman calling about Oprah
jokes.punchline: Dave dismissing Stedman's relationship with Oprah
jokes.explanation: Standard territorial behavior but fits the character's delusion well

jokes.text: Look, Stedman, you gonna have to move on, alright? No, fuck that, I don't care what y'all had. She's having my baby, okay?
jokes.setup: Phone call from Stedman
jokes.punchline: Dave aggressively claiming territory over Oprah
jokes.explanation: Good character development showing Dave's possessiveness and confrontational nature

jokes.text: Nigga looks just like Dr. Phil!
jokes.setup: Baby being born
jokes.punchline: The baby looks like Dr. Phil, not Dave
jokes.explanation: Brilliant punchline that reveals the paternity truth in the most unexpected way

jokes.text: Oprah, what about our dreams, huh? / Sorry, Dave. / What am I gonna do now? I quit my job. Shit!
jokes.setup: Dave realizing the baby isn't his
jokes.punchline: Dave facing consequences of his gold-digging behavior
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to the sketch showing consequences, solid character work and irony

jokes.text: What's reasonable doubt to a white person, you know, might not be a reasonable doubt to a black person.
jokes.setup: Discussion about believing black celebrities are guilty
jokes.punchline: Pointing out racial differences in perception of justice
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary with perfect economy, addresses real racial dynamics in justice system

jokes.text: O.J. Ain't gonna kill nobody, he's rich.
jokes.setup: Jury selection for O.J. trial
jokes.punchline: Dave's logic that rich people don't commit murder
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical point about class privilege and assumptions about crime

jokes.text: what black man don't have some bloody gloves on his property? Nigga, I got one right here.
jokes.setup: Prosecutor presenting bloody glove evidence
jokes.punchline: Dave arguing bloody gloves are normal for black men
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of evidence by suggesting it's culturally normal, then proving it

jokes.text: We would've made real orange juice out of that nigga.
jokes.setup: Discussing what would happen if O.J. hurt his family
jokes.punchline: The 'orange juice' pun about violence
jokes.explanation: Exceptionally clever wordplay that's both dark and funny, perfectly delivered

jokes.text: Fuhrman? He's a racist, it's a racist name. You're sayin', what is he a furor, German... Sound a little familiar to you?
jokes.setup: Learning about Detective Fuhrman
jokes.punchline: Connecting the name to 'Fuhrer' and German racism
jokes.explanation: Clever connection between the detective's name and historical racism

jokes.text: Exactamundo! The defense rests, sir.
jokes.setup: Being asked if minor oversights let O.J. off the hook
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic agreement with silly word choice and legal terminology
jokes.explanation: Great word choice and character consistency, good comedic timing

jokes.text: Sir, my blackness will not permit me to make a statement like that.
jokes.setup: Being asked to admit O.J. is guilty
jokes.punchline: Claiming his racial identity prevents him from agreeing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satirical line about racial solidarity that's both funny and insightful

jokes.text: Sir, my blackness will not permit me to make a statement like that.
jokes.setup: Asked to admit O.J. probably killed his wife
jokes.punchline: Treating racial solidarity as legally binding
jokes.explanation: Perfect line delivery and concept, brilliant commentary on racial dynamics and loyalty

jokes.text: Michael Jackson has many faces, none of 'em look guilty to me. You gotta look in the eyes, not the noses.
jokes.setup: Asked about Michael Jackson's guilt
jokes.punchline: Reference to Jackson's multiple plastic surgeries
jokes.explanation: Clever reference to Jackson's changing appearance while maintaining innocence

jokes.text: So, some people say that cucumbers taste better pickled. What? Huh?
jokes.setup: Being told Jackson was accused multiple times
jokes.punchline: Completely non-sequitur response about pickles
jokes.explanation: Absurdist deflection that shows Dave's character avoiding uncomfortable truths

jokes.text: There's a head, a shaft, some balls, hair, maybe pressed, permed hair, with glitter sprinkled on it.
jokes.setup: Claiming he could describe Michael Jackson's penis
jokes.punchline: The absurd description including permed hair and glitter
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd description that parodies Jackson's appearance while making a point about generic descriptions

jokes.text: Come on, dude, I couldn't pick my own penis out of a lineup, all right. And me and my penis is like this, son.
jokes.setup: Arguing that penis descriptions aren't reliable evidence
jokes.punchline: Can't even identify his own penis despite their close relationship
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-deprecating humor that makes a logical point about eyewitness reliability

jokes.text: He made Thriller... Thriller. So you'd let your children sleep with him? Fuck no! Eww.
jokes.setup: Defending Michael Jackson because he made Thriller
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting himself when asked about his own kids
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction that exposes the logical flaw in his reasoning - brilliant structure

jokes.text: So you'd let your children sleep with him. / Fuck no! Eww.
jokes.setup: Logical follow-up to Dave defending Michael
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction of his defense when it becomes personal
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection and hypocrisy reveal, shows the contradiction in blind loyalty

jokes.text: Baretta did that shit.
jokes.setup: Asked about Robert Blake facing murder charges
jokes.punchline: Immediate, matter-of-fact guilty verdict
jokes.explanation: Perfectly timed deadpan delivery that contrasts with his elaborate O.J. and Michael Jackson defenses

jokes.text: Have I seen it! Like 80 times. You know the part where he's waiting for her to come over, and he's looking in the camera like... (grunting)
jokes.setup: Asked if he's seen the R. Kelly tape
jokes.punchline: Admitting to watching it 80 times and describing specific parts
jokes.explanation: Appropriately cringey admission that shows Dave's character's hypocrisy

jokes.text: For all I know, that piss was digital. They get crazy with special effects.
jokes.setup: Defending R. Kelly despite admitting it was him
jokes.punchline: Claiming CGI urine as reasonable doubt
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd defense using modern technology

jokes.text: For all I know, that piss was digital. They get crazy with special effects.
jokes.setup: Trying to defend R. Kelly despite admitting the tape shows him
jokes.punchline: Claiming the urination was CGI
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist defense - the idea of CGI piss is hilariously ridiculous but delivered with conviction

jokes.text: you give me that kind of money, R. Kelly can fart in my dinner tonight. Spray-fart.
jokes.setup: Talking about how much money the accuser stands to make
jokes.punchline: Saying he'd let R. Kelly contaminate his food for that money
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor that makes the point about money motivating accusations, but relies on shock value

jokes.text: if I saw a tape of R. Kelly peeing on a girl while he was singing 'Piss On You'... lovers wanna love...
jokes.setup: Listing impossible requirements for conviction
jokes.punchline: Parody of R. Kelly's 'I Wish' song as 'Piss On You'
jokes.explanation: Clever parody of R. Kelly's actual song that fits perfectly with the scenario

jokes.text: That's my Robert, always peeing on people. Don't forget her hair Robert. Yes, grandmother.
jokes.setup: Requiring R. Kelly's grandmother to identify him
jokes.punchline: Grandmother casually acknowledging his habit of urinating on people
jokes.explanation: Hilariously absurd escalation with the grandmother treating urination as a normal habit

jokes.text: I've seen a tape where five cops beat up a nigga and they said that they had reasonable doubt.
jokes.setup: Defending his high standards for evidence
jokes.punchline: Reference to Rodney King beating and acquittal
jokes.explanation: Powerful satirical point about racial disparities in the justice system

jokes.text: How come they didn't find Big and Tupac's murderers but they arrest O.J. the next day? Nicole Simpson can't rap.
jokes.setup: Questioning justice system priorities
jokes.punchline: Nicole Simpson's lack of rap skills as the reason for quick arrest
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant line that makes a devastating point about racial bias in law enforcement priorities through perfect dark comedy

jokes.text: Some good-ass cheese, shit... wait... I'm chokin' on cheese.
jokes.setup: Returning to the cheese obsession from earlier
jokes.punchline: The irony of choking on the cheese he praised
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that brings the cheese obsession full circle with ironic consequences

jokes.text: Say 'yes, grandmama' to the camera. Yes, grandmama.
jokes.setup: Someone directing Dave for the camera
jokes.punchline: Reference to the R. Kelly grandmother bit
jokes.explanation: Solid callback to the earlier bit though not as strong as the original

tmdb_overview: Dave thinks Oprah is pregant with his child. Dave also goes into his experience as a member of the O.J. Simpson trial jury. Also featuring musical guest Wyclef.
